content.text: When user selects a Component Splice test (e.g., L3) from the Rules and Action test names list, system shall open a right-side configuration drawer Component Splice (L3)
a configuration drawer to open when I select the Component Splice test
I can review the rule configuration.
I am viewing the Rules and Action test names list,
the system opens a right-side configuration drawer titled “Component Splice (L3)”.
System shall display a descriptive subtitle explaining the purpose of the test (e.g., Connection integrity assessment for upright splice.)
see a descriptive subtitle explaining the test purpose
I understand what the rule evaluates.
the system displays a descriptive subtitle explaining the purpose of the test.
System shall display the test scope badge (e.g., Local) in the header.
the test scope badge displayed in the header
I know whether the rule applies globally or locally.
the system displays the test scope badge (e.g., Local).
For Component Splice (L3), system shall display Alignment Checks section.
an Alignment Checks section displayed for the Component Splice test
the Component Splice (L3) configuration drawer is open,
the system displays an Alignment Checks section.
System shall display Down-Aisle Offset (δDA) thresholds with defined severity ranges.
the Alignment Checks section is displayed,
the system displays severity ranges for Down-Aisle Offset (δDA).
System shall define Green condition for δDA as ≤ 1.0 mm.
the green condition defined for Down-Aisle Offset
I know the acceptable alignment range.
it is defined as δDA ≤ 1.0 mm.
System shall define Amber condition for δDA as > 1.0 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm.
the Amber condition defined for Down-Aisle Offset
I understand when moderate misalignment occurs.
it is defined as δDA > 1.0 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm.
System shall define Red condition for δDA as > 2.0 mm.
the Red condition defined for Down-Aisle Offset
I know when alignment is critical.
it is defined as δDA > 2.0 mm.
System shall display Cross-Aisle Offset (δ CA) thresholds with severity ranges.
I understand severity classification for cross-aisle alignment.
the Alignment Checks section is visible,
the system displays severity ranges for Cross-Aisle Offset (δCA).
System shall define Green condition for δCA as ≤ 1.0 mm.
the Green condition defined for Cross-Aisle Offset
I know the acceptable tolerance level.
it is defined as δCA ≤ 1.0 mm.

11.title: Define Amber Condition for Cross-Aisle Offset

content.text: System shall define Amber condition for δ CA as > 1.0 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm.
the Amber condition defined for Cross-Aisle Offset
I understand when moderate cross-aisle misalignment occurs.
Cross-Aisle Offset (δCA) severity thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as δCA > 1.0 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm.

12.title: Define Red Condition for Cross-Aisle Offset

content.text: System shall define Red condition for δ CA as > 2.0 mm.
the Red condition defined for Cross-Aisle Offset
I know when cross-aisle misalignment becomes critical.
Cross-Aisle Offset (δCA) severity thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as δCA > 2.0 mm.
System shall display Vertical Gap (gv) thresholds with severity ranges.
I understand the severity criteria for vertical alignment.
the Alignment Checks section is visible,
the system shows severity ranges for Vertical Gap (gv).

14.title: Define Green Condition for Vertical Gap

content.text: System shall define Green condition for Vertical gv as ≤ 1.0 mm.
the Green condition defined for Vertical Gap
Vertical Gap (gv) severity thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as gv ≤ 1.0 mm.

15.title: Define Amber Condition for Vertical Gap

content.text: System shall define Amber condition for Vertical gv as > 1.0 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm.
the Amber condition defined for Vertical Gap
I know when vertical separation requires monitoring.
Vertical Gap (gv) severity thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as gv > 1.0 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm.

16.title: Define Red Condition for Vertical Gap

content.text: System shall define Red condition for Vertical gv as > 2.0 mm.
the Red condition defined for Vertical Gap
I know when vertical separation is critical.
Vertical Gap (gv) severity thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as gv > 2.0 mm.
System shall display Connection Integrity section for Component Splice.
I can review connection-related rule logic.
the Component Splice configuration drawer is open,
the system displays a Connection Integrity section.
System shall display Bolt Torque (Tm vs Td) evaluation logic.
I understand how torque values are assessed.
the Connection Integrity section is displayed,
the system displays Bolt Torque (Tm vs Td) evaluation logic.

19.title: Define Green Condition for Bolt Torque

content.text: System shall define Green condition for torque as 0.9 × Td ≤ Tm ≤ 1.1 × Td.
the Green condition defined for bolt torque
I know when torque is within acceptable range.
Bolt Torque evaluation thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as 0.9 × Td ≤ Tm ≤ 1.1 × Td.

20.title: Define Amber Condition for Bolt Torque

content.text: System shall define Amber condition for torque as 0.8 × Td ≤ Tm < 0.9 × Td.
the Amber condition defined for bolt torque
I know when torque requires attention.
Bolt Torque evaluation thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as 0.8 × Td ≤ Tm < 0.9 × Td.

21.title: Define Red Condition for Bolt Torque

content.text: System shall define Red condition for torque as Tm < 0.8 × Td.
the Red condition defined for bolt torque
I know when torque is critically low.
Bolt Torque evaluation thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as Tm < 0.8 × Td.
System shall display Bolt Count section.
I can verify required bolt presence.
the system displays a Bolt Count section.

23.title: Define Green Condition for Bolt Count

content.text: System shall define Green condition for bolt count as Nm ≥ Nd.
the Green condition defined for bolt count
I know when all required bolts are present.
Bolt Count evaluation rules are displayed,
the system defines it as Nm ≥ Nd.

24.title: Define Red Condition for Bolt Count

content.text: System shall define Red condition for bolt count as Nm < Nd (Missing bolt).
the Red condition defined for bolt count
I know when bolts are missing.
Bolt Count evaluation rules are displayed,
the system defines it as Nm < Nd.
System shall display the Visible Crack inspection parameter under the Component Splice evaluation model.
the Visible Crack inspection parameter displayed in the Component Splice evaluation model
I can evaluate structural crack conditions.
the Component Splice evaluation model modal is open,
the system shows the Visible Crack inspection parameter.

26.title: Define Green Condition for Visible Crack

content.text: System shall display the Green status condition for Visible Crack as “No crack observed.”
the Green status condition defined for Visible Crack
I understand the acceptable inspection outcome.
the Visible Crack parameter is displayed,
the system defines the Green condition as “No crack observed.”

27.title: Define Red Condition for Visible Crack

content.text: System shall display the Red status condition for Visible Crack as “Crack visible.”
the Red status condition defined for Visible Crack
I understand when the condition is critical.
the Visible Crack parameter is displayed,
the system defines the Red condition as “Crack visible.”
System shall display the Overall Severity Logic section within the Component Splice Evaluation Model modal.
see an Overall Severity Logic section in the evaluation model
I understand how the final severity is determined.
the Component Splice Evaluation Model modal is open,
the system shows an Overall Severity Logic section.

29.title: Determine Final Severity Based on Worst Condition

content.text: System shall determine the Final severity based on the worst condition among all individual checks. (Final Severity = Worst of all individual checks.
the final severity determined using the worst condition among checks
the most critical inspection result governs the outcome.
the system calculates the final severity,
it uses the rule “Final Severity = Worst of all individual checks.”

30.title: Set Final Severity to Red When Any Parameter is Red

content.text: System shall set the Final Severity as Red if any inspection parameter is classified as Red.
the final severity set to Red if any parameter is Red
one or more inspection parameters are classified as Red,
the system determines the final severity,
the final severity is set to Red.

31.title: Set Final Severity to Amber When No Red Exists but Amber is Present

content.text: System shall set the Final Severity as Amber if no parameter if at any parameter is Amber.
the final severity set to Amber when Amber conditions exist but no Red conditions are present
no inspection parameters are Red and at least one parameter is Amber,
the system determines the final severity,
the final severity is set to Amber.

32.title: Set Final Severity to Green When All Conditions Are Green

content.text: System shall set the Final Severity as Green if all inspection parameters are classified as Green.
the final severity set to Green when all parameters are acceptable
I know the component is compliant.
all inspection parameters are classified as Green,
the system calculates the final severity,
the final severity is set to Green.
System shall display “System Defined” label in footer for non-configurable tests.
see a “System Defined” label for non-configurable tests
I understand the rule cannot be modified.
the evaluation model represents a system-defined rule,
the system shows a “System Defined” label.
System shall display the Close button in the evaluation model modal.
a Close button available in the evaluation model modal
the evaluation model modal is open,
the system provides a Close button.
System shall close the Component Splice Evaluation Model modal when the user clicks the Close button.
the modal to close when I click the Close button
I can return to the previous screen.
the evaluation model modal is open,
System shall allow the user to dismiss the modal using the top-right close (X) icon.
dismiss the modal using the close (X) icon
I can quickly exit the modal.
the evaluation model modal is open,
I click the top-right close (X) icon,

37.title: Return to Previous Screen After Modal Dismissal

content.text: System shall cancel the modal view and return the user to the previous screen when the modal is dismissed using Close or X action.
the system to return me to the previous screen after closing the modal
I can continue working in the Rules and Actions list.
the evaluation model modal is open,
I dismiss it using the Close button or close (X) icon,
the system cancels the modal view and returns to the previous screen.
